description: A 3-Dimensional problem. Please flag and/or comment if there is a mistake. Also see the next problem, 22364.
sgf: (;SZ[5]AB[aa]TR[ca][cb][ac][bc][cc][dc][ec]LB[ba:A][bb:B][eb:C]C[3-Dimensional go (2x2x2)! A5, B5, A4, and B4 make up the top 2x2 layer; D5, E5, D4, and E4 (respectively) make up the bottom. Some moves are restricted due to symmetry. White to play to get the best result under Japanese rules.FORCE](;W[ba];B[bb]TR[ca][cb][ac][bc][cc][dc][ec]SQ[ea]C[Since this is 3-D go, your stone is in atari and can only be saved by playing at E5. FORCE];W[ea];B[eb]TR[ca][cb][ac][bc][cc][dc][ec]SQ[da]C[Black ataris again. FORCE];W[da];B[db]TR[ca][cb][ac][bc][cc][dc][ec]LB[ba:X][da:X][ea:X]C[White runs, but black kills anyway (White's group has no more liberties).])(;W[eb];B[bb]TR[ca][cb][ac][bc][cc][dc][ec]SQ[ea][db]C[If black could play D4 or E5, that would make an eye at A4 or B5. White wants to prevent this. There is only one move that prevents both.];W[da]TR[ca][cb][ac][bc][cc][dc][ec]C[Correct! Seki and jigo, the best result. RIGHT])(;W[bb];B[ea]TR[ca][cb][ac][bc][cc][dc][ec]C[Correct so far. Now what?](;W[ab];B[db]TR[ca][cb][ac][bc][cc][dc][ec]C[Now black has an eye at D5, and white has no way to make an eye, so black wins.])(;W[ba];B[db]TR[ca][cb][ac][bc][cc][dc][ec]C[Now black has an eye at D5, and white has no way to make an eye, so black wins.])(;W[da];B[db]TR[ca][cb][ac][bc][cc][dc][ec]LB[da:X]C[The stone at D5 is captured. Now black has an eye at D5, and white has no way to make an eye, so black wins.])(;W[eb];B[db]TR[ca][cb][ac][bc][cc][dc][ec]C[Now black has an eye at D5, and white has no way to make an eye, so black wins.])(;W[db]TR[ca][cb][ac][bc][cc][dc][ec]C[Seki and jigo, the best result.])))
